requirements.description: Proposals must address next-generation AI agents as autonomous systems powered by large AI language models (e.g., large language models or large multimodal models) that can plan, utilise tools, and perform actions autonomously to achieve specified goals based on high-level instructions.
Effective AI agent design must incorporate robust planning, reasoning, and search mechanisms that allow agents to approach complex tasks by breaking them down into structured subgoals.
Proposals must address effective memory and state management as a necessary component, achieved through a balanced integration of short-term and external long-term memory solutions.
Integrating external tools and APIs is essential for overcoming limitations in large AI models and enhancing agent performance in tasks requiring accuracy and reliability.
All proposals must incorporate mechanisms for assessing and demonstrating progress, including qualitative and quantitative KPIs, benchmarking, and progress monitoring.
Communicable results must be shared with the European R&D community through the AI-on-demand platform.
Projects must link to the resources offered by the AI Factories, including the Data Labs.
All proposals must allocate tasks for cohesion activities with the co-programmed European Partnership on AI, Data and Robotics (ADRA) and the CSA HORIZON-CL4-2025-03-HUMAN-18: GenAI4EU central Hub.
Projects will contribute to the Apply AI strategy.
Potential research area: enhancing AI agent autonomy through advanced self-planning and self-optimization capabilities, enabling agents to improve their decision-making and strategic planning.
Potential research area: innovation in memory-augmented AI agents to facilitate robust long-term reasoning and lifelong learning.
Potential research area: developing advanced multi-agent frameworks specifically tailored for collaborative agents, including research on AI agent frameworks based on mixed AI architectures.
Potential research area: advancing multimodal reasoning capabilities to enable real-world applications.
When possible, proposals should build on and reuse public results from relevant previous funded actions.
Results may be validated in the Testing and Experiment Facilities and further deployed via the European Digital Innovation Hubs (EDIHs).
Proposals should build on or seek collaboration with relevant projects and develop synergies with other relevant International, European, national, or regional initiatives.
To support portfolio coverage, individual proposals may focus on one of three designated application sectors: healthcare, advanced manufacturing, or in-vehicle autonomous driving.
